architecture.name: CombPilot64_CFR_Estimation_Suite
architecture.summary: Estimate full 64-tone CFR from 16 comb pilots (spacing 4) using three classical estimators with increasing complexity: LS+linear/spline interpolation, DFT delay-domain truncation after LS interpolation, and covariance-aided LMMSE Wiener reconstruction.
architecture.rationale: The channel is frequency-correlated (small RMS delay spread) and delay-compressible under CP. Hence interpolation is a low-complexity baseline, DFT truncation adds model-based denoising, and LMMSE exploits second-order statistics for best NMSE at higher compute cost while keeping inversion in pilot domain (16x16).
architecture.algorithm_structure: Single Monte-Carlo sweep framework: generate TDL-A channels and pilot observations per SNR; compute all estimators on each trial; accumulate NMSE on non-pilot tones, confidence bands, runtime, and complexity proxies; plot trade-offs.
architecture.pseudocode:
1) Define OFDM and pilot indices: N=64, P={0,4,...,60}, D=all\P. Build SNR grid 0:2:30.
2) For each SNR point and trial, generate delay-domain taps \(h[\ell]\sim\mathcal{CN}(0,p_\ell)\), then CFR \(H[k]=\sum_{\ell=0}^{L_h-1} h[\ell]e^{-j2\pi k\ell/N}\).
3) Form pilot observations via \(\mathbf{y}_p=\mathbf{X}_p\mathbf{h}_p+\mathbf{n}_p\), with \(|X_p|=1\), \(\mathbf{n}_p\sim\mathcal{CN}(0,\sigma_n^2\mathbf{I})\).
4) Compute LS pilot estimate \(\hat{\mathbf{h}}_p^{\mathrm{LS}}=\mathbf{X}_p^{-1}\mathbf{y}_p\).
5) Baseline A (linear): reconstruct full band by piecewise interpolation \(\hat H_{\mathrm{lin}}[k]=\mathcal{I}_{\mathrm{lin}}\{(k_{p,m},\hat H_p^{LS}[m])\}(k)\).
6) Baseline B (spline): reconstruct by cubic basis \(\hat H_{\mathrm{spline}}[k]=\sum_m \hat H_p^{LS}[m]\,\beta_3((k-k_{p,m})/\Delta k)\).
7) Method C (DFT-truncated): \(\tilde h[n]=\frac{1}{N}\sum_{k=0}^{N-1}\hat H_{\mathrm{lin}}[k]e^{j2\pi kn/N}\), truncate \(\hat h_T[n]=\tilde h[n]\mathbf{1}_{0\le n<L_t}\), then \(\hat H_{\mathrm{DFT}}[k]=\sum_{n=0}^{N-1}\hat h_T[n]e^{-j2\pi kn/N}\).
8) Method D (LMMSE Wiener): build covariance \([\mathbf{R}_{HH}]_{m,n}=\sum_{\ell}p_\ell e^{-j2\pi(m-n)\ell/N}\), then \(\hat{\mathbf{H}}_{\mathrm{LMMSE}}=\mathbf{R}_{Hp}(\mathbf{R}_{pp}+\sigma_n^2\mathbf{I})^{-1}\hat{\mathbf{h}}_p^{LS}\).
9) Compute non-pilot NMSE per method: \(\mathrm{NMSE}=\frac{\|\hat{\mathbf{H}}_D-\mathbf{H}_D\|_2^2}{\|\mathbf{H}_D\|_2^2}\), then \(\mathrm{NMSE}_{dB}=10\log_{10}(\frac{1}{T}\sum_t\mathrm{NMSE}_t)\).
10) Store mean, std, 95% CI \(\bar x\pm1.96\,s/\sqrt{T}\), runtime, and complexity proxies; plot curves and sensitivity studies.
architecture.estimated_complexity: Per OFDM symbol: LS+linear O(N), LS+spline O(N), DFT-trunc O(N log N), LMMSE O(N_p^3 + N N_p) with N_p=16 (pilot-domain inversion only).
architecture.potential_weakness: LMMSE depends on covariance/PDP accuracy; DFT truncation is sensitive to assumed tap length L_t; spline can overshoot near edges.

algorithm_spec.objective: Recover full-band CFR \(\hat{\mathbf{H}}\in\mathbb{C}^{64}\) from noisy comb pilots and minimize non-pilot NMSE across SNR sweep while reporting runtime/complexity trade-offs.

inputs.name: snr_db_grid
inputs.description: SNR points from 0 to 30 dB with 2 dB step.

inputs.name: ofdm_params
inputs.description: Contains N=64, Np=16, pilot_spacing=4, CP=16, pilot indices, data indices.

inputs.name: tdl_pdp
inputs.description: Normalized TDL-A tap powers used for channel generation and covariance construction.

inputs.name: assumed_trunc_len
inputs.description: Delay-domain truncation length L_t for DFT-based method.

outputs.name: nmse_db_curves
outputs.description: Mean NMSE in dB on non-pilot tones for each method.

outputs.name: nmse_ci95_db
outputs.description: Lower/upper 95% CI of NMSE_dB or CI band from linear-scale NMSE.

outputs.name: runtime_ms_per_symbol
outputs.description: Average runtime per OFDM symbol estimate.

outputs.name: example_reconstructions
outputs.description: Sample true/estimated CFR and delay profiles for visualization.

pipeline.name: Pilot-data observation model
pipeline.purpose: Construct noisy pilot observations from true CFR.
pipeline.implementation_hint: Use complex Gaussian noise via np.random.randn + 1j*np.random.randn scaled by noise variance.

pipeline.name: LS estimation on pilot tones
pipeline.implementation_hint: Elementwise divide y_p / x_p using NumPy broadcasting.

pipeline.name: LS linear interpolation (low-complexity baseline)
pipeline.purpose: Reconstruct full CFR using piecewise linear interpolation from pilot LS values.
pipeline.implementation_hint: Use np.interp on real/imag parts separately, then recombine.

pipeline.name: LS spline interpolation baseline
pipeline.purpose: Higher-order interpolation baseline with moderate complexity.
pipeline.implementation_hint: Implement cubic interpolation with local 4-point polynomial fit (np.polyfit) per segment, real/imag separately.

pipeline.name: DFT delay-domain truncation after LS interpolation
pipeline.purpose: Denoise CFR by enforcing finite channel support.
pipeline.formula_latex: \tilde h[n]=\frac{1}{N}\sum_{k=0}^{N-1}\hat H_{\mathrm{lin}}[k]e^{j2\pi kn/N},\;\hat h_T[n]=\tilde h[n]\mathbf{1}_{0\le n<L_t},\;\hat H_{\mathrm{DFT}}[k]=\sum_{n=0}^{N-1}\hat h_T[n]e^{-j2\pi kn/N}
pipeline.implementation_hint: Use np.fft.ifft, zero taps from L_t onward, then np.fft.fft.

pipeline.name: Covariance-aided LMMSE Wiener interpolation
pipeline.purpose: Use TDL-based second-order statistics for minimum MSE linear reconstruction.
pipeline.implementation_hint: Build Toeplitz-like covariance via vectorized phase differences; invert 16x16 matrix with np.linalg.solve.

pipeline.name: Non-pilot NMSE and confidence bands
pipeline.purpose: Quantify reconstruction quality on the 48 data subcarriers.
pipeline.implementation_hint: Accumulate per-trial linear NMSE arrays; use np.mean, np.std; convert to dB for reporting.

core_assumptions: Block fading within one OFDM symbol.
Perfect synchronization and known unit-modulus pilots.
TDL-A PDP is known for simulation; covariance is available for LMMSE.
implementation_constraints: Any explicit inversion limited to pilot-domain size <=16x16.
Evaluate all required methods in the same Monte-Carlo/SNR loop.
failure_modes: Wrong truncation length L_t causes bias or noise leakage in DFT method.
Cubic interpolation edge effects can increase error near band boundaries.
Too few trials can produce unstable CI bands.

primary_metrics.name: NMSE_dB_nonpilot
primary_metrics.description: Main recovery metric on the 48 non-pilot tones.

secondary_metrics.name: runtime_per_symbol_ms
secondary_metrics.description: Average wall-clock runtime per estimate for each method.

secondary_metrics.name: complexity_flops_proxy
secondary_metrics.description: Analytical complexity proxy to compare algorithmic cost.

secondary_metrics.name: nmse_gain_over_ls_linear_dB
secondary_metrics.description: Difference in NMSE_dB relative to LS linear baseline.

baseline_methods.name: LS_linear_interpolation
baseline_methods.implementation_hint: np.interp over pilot grid for real/imag parts.

baseline_methods.name: LS_spline_interpolation
baseline_methods.implementation_hint: Local cubic polynomial interpolation with np.polyfit.

baseline_methods.name: DFT_truncated_LS
baseline_methods.description: Model-aided denoising using finite delay support.
baseline_methods.key_formula_latex: \tilde h[n]=\mathrm{IFFT}\{\hat H_{\mathrm{lin}}[k]\},\;\hat h_T[n]=\tilde h[n]\mathbf{1}_{0\le n<L_t},\;\hat H_{\mathrm{DFT}}[k]=\mathrm{FFT}\{\hat h_T[n]\}

baseline_methods.name: LMMSE_Wiener_frequency_interpolation
baseline_methods.description: Covariance-driven best expected NMSE with pilot-domain inversion.
baseline_methods.implementation_hint: Precompute covariance matrices per scenario; solve 16x16 linear systems with np.linalg.solve.

sensitivity_factors.description: Quantifies bias-variance trade-off in delay-domain truncation.
Tests interpolation difficulty and covariance reconstruction quality as pilot spacing changes.
Controls CFR smoothness and coherence bandwidth; directly impacts interpolation error.

plots.title: NMSE vs SNR on non-pilot tones
plots.description: Primary performance comparison with CI bands.

plots.title: Sensitivity: DFT truncation length impact
plots.description: Shows robustness/mismatch sensitivity of delay-domain prior.

boundary_conditions.name: High SNR limit (30 dB)
boundary_conditions.description: Noise is weak; model mismatch dominates.
boundary_conditions.expected_behavior: LMMSE and DFT approach true-channel floor; LS interpolation plateaus higher.

boundary_conditions.name: Low SNR limit (0 dB)
boundary_conditions.description: Pilot estimates are heavily noise corrupted.
boundary_conditions.expected_behavior: LMMSE best due to Wiener shrinkage; DFT better than pure interpolation; spline may overfit noise.

boundary_conditions.name: Truncation mismatch
boundary_conditions.description: Assumed L_t too small or too large.
boundary_conditions.expected_behavior: Too small increases bias; too large increases variance/noise leakage.

notebook_plan.purpose: State task, methods, and expected outputs.
must_include: Problem statement (64-tone OFDM, 16 comb pilots, recover 48 non-pilot tones)
NMSE vs SNR objective and confidence bands
TDL-A, RMS delay spread reference 30 ns

notebook_plan.purpose: Present core equations used in implementation.
Load required libraries and set reproducibility.
Implement TDL-A tap generation, CFR construction, and noisy pilot observation simulator.
Implement all estimators in notebook functions.
Create sweep function looping over every SNR point and optionally sensitivity factors; compute metrics independently per point and per method.
must_include: evaluate all methods in same loop
support optional factor argument (L_t, pilot count, delay spread)

notebook_plan.purpose: Run default experiment and sensitivity experiments.
must_include: execute at least one sensitivity sweep
NMSE vs SNR with CI bands
Which method wins NMSE and where
Observed sensitivity to L_t and pilot density

algorithm_steps.name: DFT delay-domain truncation after LS interpolation
algorithm_steps.formula_latex: \tilde h[n]=\frac{1}{N}\sum_{k=0}^{N-1}\hat H_{\mathrm{lin}}[k]e^{j2\pi kn/N},\;\hat h_T[n]=\tilde h[n]\mathbf{1}_{0\le n<L_t},\;\hat H_{\mathrm{DFT}}[k]=\sum_{n=0}^{N-1}\hat h_T[n]e^{-j2\pi kn/N}

implementation_constraints: Any explicit inversion limited to pilot-domain size <=16x16.
Evaluate all required methods in the same Monte-Carlo/SNR loop.
